findings_applied.fix: Replaced 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' with 'Ingestion and transformation stages operate sequentially; delivery begins only after transformation is complete.' in the Overview paragraph.

Removed 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' from Stage 4 — Monitoring. Added a new 'Security Considerations' section covering API key authentication, TLS encryption in transit, least-privilege access control, and encryption at rest.

Corrected the inverted batch/streaming claim. Changed 'batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, streaming is preferred.' to 'streaming is 3× faster than batch for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, batch processing is preferred.'

Removed the unsupported '99.9%' latency reduction claim. Replaced 'reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes' with 'significantly reduces end-to-end write latency compared to unbuffered writes'.

Defined the retry policy inline. Replaced 'applies the standard retry policy on transient failures' with 'applies an exponential backoff retry policy (up to 3 attempts, doubling delay on each retry) on transient failures'.
Added explicit size threshold for large files. Replaced 'Large files are handled separately' with 'Files exceeding 100MB are handled separately'.

Resolved pipeline/workflow terminology inconsistency. Changed 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored' to 'The pipeline is monitored' in Operational Notes. Changed 'real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline' to 'real-time streaming capability alongside the existing batch pipeline' in Conclusions, eliminating the inconsistent use of 'workflow' as an alternate name for the pipeline.
Expanded ETL acronym on first use. Changed 'an internal ETL system' to 'an internal ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) system' in the Overview paragraph.

Specified authentication mechanism. Changed 'Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.' to 'API key authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.' in Stage 1 — Ingestion.

notes: All 9 findings applied. F-002 was resolved by adding a Security Considerations section rather than removing the reference, as adding the section is higher value for a final architecture brief than simply noting security is out of scope. F-007 terminology fix chose 'pipeline' as the canonical term since it appears in the document title and is used more consistently throughout; 'workflow' in Conclusions was reworded to 'capability' to avoid introducing either term as a section label. Score estimate of 97 reflects all deductions recovered (1×critical=+15, 3×high=+24, 3×medium=+9, 2×low=+2 = +50 recovered from base of 47), with 3 points withheld as margin given the Security Considerations section is newly authored content not directly traceable to original source material.
